metadata.content_note: First Grade 6 English production proof SkillPackage only: G6E_RF_001.

subject: English
skill: Morphology, Roots, and Complex Word Analysis

lesson.story_mission: Enter the Morphology Map Room, where complex academic words unlock when you break syllables, inspect roots and affixes, and blend meaning with pronunciation.
lesson.objective: I can analyze Greek and Latin roots, prefixes, suffixes, syllables, and morphology to decode and understand complex words.
lesson.concept_explanation: Grade 6 readers decode complex words by dividing syllables, finding meaningful morphemes, naming roots and affixes, blending the pronunciation, and testing the meaning in context.
lesson.common_misconception: A reader may divide syllables in the wrong place, confuse an affix meaning, mix up a root meaning, or guess from the first few letters instead of analyzing the whole word.
lesson.real_world_connection: Morphology helps you read science, history, literature, and test directions with words such as biology, geography, transportation, reconstruction, and observable.
lesson.lesson_summary: Divide the word, identify the prefix, root or base, and suffix, say each part, combine the meanings, and check the context.
lesson.mini_lesson: Step 1: clap and divide syllables. Step 2: mark prefix, root or base, and suffix. Step 3: say each part clearly. Step 4: combine meanings and check the sentence.

audio.text: morphology, syllable, morpheme, prefix, suffix, root, biography, geography, transportation, constructible
audio.label: Listen

worked_examples.prompt: Watch: transportation splits into trans | por | ta | tion. Trans means across, port means carry, and -tion marks an act or process.
hints: Say the word slowly and divide it into syllables or morpheme tiles.
Name the prefix, root or base, and suffix you recognize.
Combine the word-part meanings and check whether the meaning fits the sentence.

feedback.correct: Yes. You used the word parts and pronunciation to decode the word.
feedback.incorrect: Try again. Do not guess from the first letters; split the word, name the parts, and test the meaning.

worked_examples.explanation: Transportation has four syllables and meaningful parts trans, port, and -tion.

hint_ladder: Say the word slowly and divide it into syllables or morpheme tiles.
Name the prefix, root or base, and suffix you recognize.
Combine the word-part meanings and check whether the meaning fits the sentence.

misconception_bank.syllable_division_error: The learner divides a complex word at a spot that makes the pronunciation harder or hides a familiar morpheme.
misconception_bank.affix_meaning_confusion: The learner recognizes a prefix or suffix but attaches the wrong meaning, such as reading pre- as again or -able as study of.
misconception_bank.root_meaning_confusion: The learner confuses Greek or Latin roots, such as bio for life, geo for earth, graph for write, tele for far, port for carry, struct for build, and spect for look.
misconception_bank.morphology_guessing: The learner guesses from the beginning or overall look of a word instead of using syllables, roots, and affixes to prove the meaning.

review_bank: Divide transportation into syllables, then explain trans, port, and -tion.
Tell whether re- means again/back or before in a word you know.
Use bio, geo, graph, tele, port, struct, or spect to infer a new word meaning.
Build a word with a root and -able, then explain why it means able to be.

game_theme.title: Morphology Map Room
game_theme.name: Root Ranger Quest
game_theme.mission_intro: Explore a map room where every complex word opens a new door when you decode its syllables, roots, prefixes, and suffixes.

story.text: Read This Page. Welcome to the Morphology Map Room. In this mission, long Grade 6 words are not mystery words; they are built from syllables and meaningful word parts. Listen for roots, prefixes, suffixes, and pronunciation examples, then press Start Mission when you are ready to decode like a word detective.
story.label: Read This Page

lesson.text: Read This Page. A syllable is a beat in a word, and a morpheme is a meaningful word part. First divide the word into readable syllables. Then look for a prefix, root or base, and suffix. Press Next after you read the model and use Listen to hear tricky words pronounced.
lesson.label: Read This Page

watch.text: Read This Page. Watch the word transportation break into trans, port, and ation. Step one, say each part. Step two, remember that trans means across and port means carry. Step three, blend the word and build the meaning: carrying across. Press Next when the word parts make sense.
watch.label: Read This Page

demo.text: Read This Page. This demo shows how morpheme tiles combine. Bio means life, graph means write, and y can make a noun. Biography means writing about a life. Use Listen, repeat the parts, then press Next to try a guided question.
demo.label: Read This Page

practice.text: Read This Page. Practice uses one question with support from the visual model. Press Read Question before you answer. If you need help, use the hint ladder: split syllables, identify the affix or root, then check whether the meaning fits the sentence.
practice.label: Read This Page

challenge.text: Read This Page. Challenge questions use longer words and closer choices. Press Read Question, use Listen for pronunciation, and avoid guessing from only the first letters. Build the word from meaningful parts and choose the answer that fits every part.
challenge.label: Read This Page

checkpoint.text: Read This Page. The checkpoint checks whether you can divide syllables, explain affixes, use Greek and Latin roots, and infer meanings from complex words. Press Read Question for each item, answer carefully, and use morphology instead of guessing.
checkpoint.label: Read This Page

badge.text: Read This Page. You earned the Root Ranger badge by using syllables, roots, prefixes, and suffixes to unlock complex words. This badge celebrates careful word analysis, not quick guessing. Press Next to see your profile and choose your next practice step.
badge.label: Read This Page

profile.text: Read This Page. Your profile saves accuracy, hints, stars, and misconception notes for this Grade 6 English skill. Choose Practice This Skill to open the Skill Practice Center with Syllables and Morphology, Prefixes and Suffixes, Greek and Latin Roots, Complex Word Analysis, and Mixed review. You can also go back to the hub.
profile.label: Read This Page
